{"step_by_step_thinking": "Olaparib is a PARP inhibitor that has been shown to be effective in prostate cancer, particularly in patients with specific genetic mutations. Multiple documents confirm that olaparib is approved for the treatment of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer with homologous recombination repair (HRR) gene alterations, including BRCA1, BRCA2, and other DNA damage repair gene (DDRG) mutations. Document [3] states that olaparib is approved as monotherapy for any HRR gene alteration and in combination with abiraterone for BRCA-mutated disease. Document [1] notes that olaparib showed a median survival benefit of about 5 months compared to enzalutamide or abiraterone alone and was most effective in patients with BRCA2 mutations. Document [2] confirms that patients with BRCA1, BRCA2, or ATM mutations that have progressed despite hormonal therapy benefit from PARP inhibitors like olaparib. Document [10] specifies that olaparib can be used in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer with BRCA or other DNA damage repair gene mutations where the malignancy has progressed. These findings consistently support the effectiveness of olaparib in specific subsets of prostate cancer patients. Therefore, based on the evidence, olaparib is effective for prostate cancer in eligible patients.", "answer_choice": "A"}
